claimText: Project Cheetah is implemented by the National Tiger Conservation Authority (NTCA) with technical assistance from the Wildlife Institute of India (WII).
evidenceExcerpt: Implemented by NTCA with WII's technical assistance
examUse: Statement-based question on the implementing agency of Project Cheetah.
distractorHooks: Implemented by MoEFCC directly without NTCA

claimText: The Asiatic Cheetah is classified as Critically Endangered by the IUCN, whereas the African Cheetah is classified as Vulnerable.
evidenceExcerpt: Asiatic Cheetah: IUCN Critically Endangered... African Cheetah: IUCN Vulnerable
examUse: Correct-pair or statement-based question comparing conservation statuses.

claimText: The Asiatic Cheetah is currently found only in Iran and has been extinct in India since 1952.
evidenceExcerpt: found only in Iran; extinct in India since 1952.
examUse: Statement-based question on the current natural habitat of the Asiatic Cheetah.

claimText: Cheetahs are the world's fastest mammals and, unlike other big cats, they do not roar.
evidenceExcerpt: Cheetahs are the world's fastest mammals and do not roar unlike other big cats.
distractorHooks: Cheetahs are the second fastest mammals

claimText: Gandhi Sagar Wildlife Sanctuary in Madhya Pradesh features the River Chambal, Khathiar-Gir dry deciduous forests, and Chaturbhuj Nala rock shelters.
evidenceExcerpt: Gandhi Sagar WLS: Located in MP... features Khathiar-Gir dry deciduous forest, River Chambal, and Chaturbhuj Nala rock shelters.
examUse: Location-feature matching or statement-based question on protected areas.
